storyInfo: While investigating a robbery, Lin Qingyan retrieves a stone for a dying old man, one that has accompanied him his entire life. After losing and regaining the stone, the old man thinks back on his fated encounters with that collector, scattered throughout his life.

attributes.content: Oh, wow... Now that's a pretty stone.
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: Ooh, it's got such a beautiful pattern, and it's really smooth. If I carve it a bit, I might be able to sell it at the market for a good price...
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: Say, my young friend, could you give that stone back to me?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Back to you? I found this stone in the river, what do you mean, back to you?
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: It's a stone I wanted to add to my garden that took me quite a lot of effort to find. I accidentally left it here as I was returning back.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: If you give it back to me, then when my garden is restored, you can play there. How does that sound?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: No, no, no. It's yours just because you said it's yours? I saw the stone here, and I picked it up first. That means it's mine.
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: *sigh* Such an unreasonable little rascal...
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Alright, then how about this? I'll trade you some other treasure for that stone.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Name your price. Anything for the stone.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Really...? Then I want a hundred, no, a thousand...?
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: Forget hundreds or thousands. Millions, billions, enough fame and fortune to sustain you until you're old and gray will be yours, just like that.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: You...
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: Do we have a deal?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: No, no, no, the answer's still no.
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: People are always trying to take advantage. If you're okay with paying that much for this stone, it means it must be worth way more than that.
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: So I'll take real good care of it. I want to see what makes this stone so special.
attributes.name: Mischievous Kid

attributes.content: What a clever little one...
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Very well, I'm in no hurry.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: When you change your mind, I'll come find you to buy that stone.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Ridiculous... Absolutely ridiculous!
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: The old man's making trouble even on his deathbed!
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Don't say that, brother. Dad's condition is getting worse by the day, and soon...
attributes.name: Refined Woman

attributes.content: We haven't had the time to be the most filial children. He only has the one final wish, and we should do our best to make him happy.
attributes.name: Refined Woman

attributes.content: Our sister's words are much kinder than yours, little brother.
attributes.name: Reserved Man

attributes.content: Filial piety is the greatest of virtues. You might be rich now, but do not forget your roots.
attributes.name: Reserved Man

attributes.content: Yes, yes, yes, the two of you speak so highly of filial piety, but who was it who actually took care of him when he became ill?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Calling in everyone because of a lost stone. If I went to Baizao to report it, the Judicial Review Office would have me locked up for obstructing official business. What am I supposed to do, huh?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Besides... Have we ever had such a stone in our house before?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Who knows? As the second young master of this family, surely you are the most aware of how many properties we own?
attributes.name: Reserved Man

attributes.content: Me? Pah! Stop trying to act all high and mighty.
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: You've never come home once these past few years, then the moment Dad got ill, you came running back like a dutiful son. Who do you think you're trying to fool?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Alright, enough arguing. If you can't help, then don't cause trouble, okay?
attributes.name: Refined Woman

attributes.content: That Tianshi has only just arrived. Let's wait for her to find that stone for us.
attributes.name: Refined Woman

attributes.content: Master, this is a Tianshi from the Tianshi Bureaus, and a former deputy minister at the Judicial Review Office.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: She is here to help you find that stone. Are you willing to tell her about what happened to that treasure of yours?
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: ...Hmph...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: There's barely any devotion in those children of mine...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Tell them that, if my stone is not found, they will not be seeing a single fen of my money.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Whomsoever finds that stone for me, I shall bestow upon them half my estates.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: So you are the venerable Mr. Wan.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: What sort of stone is it that you lost?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: It is a stone I plucked from the river when I was young, one unique across all of Terra.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: What I meant was, what sort of treasure was this stone? Was it jade, or some other precious mineral?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Neither... It was merely an ordinary stone.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Then do you know who would desire this stone?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I do... but the one who desires it would never steal it...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Who is it? And how do you know—
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Qingyan has many more questions she wishes to ask, but the old man on the bed turns his head away, unwilling to speak another word.
Where was this stone normally kept? When was the last time the residents saw it, and at what hour?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: It is normally within a small box the master had specially made, which was hidden in the deepest storeroom in the house.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: The evening before last, I was cleaning that storeroom, and everything was as it should. The next morning, I found the box smashed against the ground, and the stone inside gone...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Can I trouble you to show me where the stone was lost?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Come, come, please, come in! Today, I have prepared some meager wine, so as to offer you my deepest gratitude. If you are unsatisfied, please, I beg your forgiveness!
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: Congratulations on your new home, good sir! May your fortunes and profits be grand! Here, a fowlbeast from the family coop, along with two baskets of eggs—
attributes.name: Passionate Farmer

attributes.content: We're from the same clan and generation, cousin. We might not have been close in the past, but why be strangers now? Please, call me Brother Wan.
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: Alright then... Brother Wan! Then take these as well. Shoes my mother worked on through the night. Please, don't mind how they look.
attributes.name: Passionate Farmer

attributes.content: She says she knows how much you've rushed about doing business these past few years, crossing hill and stream. The boots you buy in town might be sturdy, but they can't beat the comfort of homemade shoes.
attributes.name: Passionate Farmer

attributes.content: Once I've settled down, I will definitely go to visit Auntie. When my home was washed away by a deluge when I was young, she trekked several hundred li to give me two sacks of millet, despite her bad back... I never forgot.
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: *sigh* All you had left was a single stone. The adults thought you didn't have long to live, but nobody could've expected you were tougher than rock! You're really something, making it through all that!
attributes.name: Passionate Farmer

attributes.content: You made a living with your own two hands, enduring a life of hardship without complaint. You crawled your way out of the backwater, started up a business, gotten married... You've got my respect!
attributes.name: Passionate Farmer

attributes.content: It's all just luck, really...
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: But since I was forced into such a situation, not charging headlong into the future wasn't an option. I couldn't just lay down and die, could I? I'm truly lucky to have grasped my life back. I can hardly give up now.
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: The youth holds his hand to his chest, his fingers clenched tight around the stone hanging at his neck.
Now please, sit! If we keep chatting, the braised hock will all be gone!
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: No rush, everyone, there are plenty of seats for all—Heaven above, is it really you?!
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: Yes, it's me. I've come again.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: It's been quite a while. What're you here for today? Surely not to try and buy this stone again?
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: You're on the mark, I'm afraid.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Since you've already settled down, started both a family and a business... Are you willing to give me that stone now?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Naturally, the answer is... still no!
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: Putting aside the fact that this stone has saved my life, just wearing it to bed lets me sleep easy.
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: It's my lucky charm, so how could I give it to you? It simply will not do. Just give up already.
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: Very well then.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Huh? You're actually giving up?
attributes.name: Spirited Youth

attributes.content: No, of course not.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I still dearly adore that stone, but I cannot seize that which another loves.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I shall wait once more, wait until you wish to put it down, at which time I shall take it away.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: This is the storeroom where valuable assets are kept?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Yes, the master kept his important account books and deeds here. Not just anyone is allowed in.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Did anyone enter on the night the stone was lost?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Unfortunately, there was a torrential downpour that night. If I remember correctly, I never saw a light turn on in this storeroom, nor did I hear any sound coming from it.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Who normally has access to this room?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: There are altogether four copies of the key to the room and the cabinets. The master's three children each have a copy, while I keep the master's copy in his stead.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: But the stone's box has its own key. The master keeps the only copy on his person at all times.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: And the box was kept on top of that standing cabinet?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The master normally does not allow others to touch the stone. After becoming bedridden a few years ago, he has not had the time to fiddle with it either, so it has been placed somewhere out of the way.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Account books, deeds... Someone who knows what these are worth...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: If a thief came in from outside, would they have sneaked over here? This residence has many more valuables that would have caught their eye.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Pardon, did you say something?
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Nothing, just thinking about a case I was recently looking into, but I doubt it has any relevance to this.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Right, there's something I'd like to ask. Master Wan's three children, what sort of people are they?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Well...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: No matter what may have happened, I am merely a butler. It would be inappropriate for me to discuss the young masters' and young mistress's affairs...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Please, be at ease. I am not asking you to judge them as people, but rather simply asking what their day-to-day work is, and under what circumstances they come to visit Master Wan. That is all.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: This is for the sake of your master's final wish. You may speak freely.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: *sigh*...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: When it comes to the master's three children, if you were to only look at their achievements, they would all show great promise.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: The elder young master, oldest of them all, is a scholar. He has always boasted impressive grades, and currently holds a post at the Academy.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: The second child, the younger young master, took after his father, and has recently brought much custom to the family businesses.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: The youngest, the young mistress, is uninterested in such mundanity, dedicating herself to music. She is a musician renowned far and wide.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Based on your tone, it sounds like they have their hidden flaws?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I dare not say, I dare not... Who would care for the opinion of a lowly servant such as I?
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: But... from my bystander's point of view, these three children are not particularly close to the master.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: The three of them moved out when they came of age, rarely returning even during festivities...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Does Master Wan have a bad relationship with his children?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: *sigh* Another long story, I'm afraid.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: When the master was but a shepherd boy, he had nothing. It was only after much bitter toil that he was able to start the family businesses.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: But when it came to the care of his children, he found himself willing but unable.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: It isn't hard to imagine.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Tianshi Lin, surely you do not suspect...?
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: I simply understand the situation a little better.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: By the way, why does your master value this stone so? Did he plan to sell it?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: That has never been the case, as far as I know.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: That stone is the master's most precious treasure. We staff normally never have the opportunity to see it.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: I once suggested taking it to the jade store to be appraised, but he scolded me with a great fury in return. I have never dared mention it again ever since.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: I do remember one thing, though. The master once mentioned a strange individual thinking of buying the stone, one who has been thinking for several decades now.
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Several decades...?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Your master mentioned finding this stone in the river several decades ago...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Does this place still exist?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I'll be off now, master...
attributes.name: Tired Farmer

attributes.content: Right. Do I still owe you any wages? If so, I'll think of a way to—
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: It's all settled. The beams and pillars that survived the fire were made of top-rate cedarwood. The coffinmaker's already gathered them up. The money that's left over... I put it on the stove.
attributes.name: Tired Farmer

attributes.content: Then... Then you can go now. And there's no need to call me 'master' anymore.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: Brother Wan... You've done lots of good deeds, enough that nobody would think you any less virtuous than anyone else. How did it all go so wrong...?
attributes.name: Tired Farmer

attributes.content: I didn't do all that just to cultivate virtue and show off.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: Who would've thought your good name would make you a target for vicious robbers! Damn it all, how can Heaven be so blind?! Letting such evil men go free just makes it harder for us honest folk!
attributes.name: Tired Farmer

attributes.content: Alright, alright... That's enough of that.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I'm halfway to being old and gray, my family and funds scattered to the four winds. Being able to hold on to my life and... this stone... means I'm still rather lucky.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: The middle-aged man holds a wooden box tight to his chest. The blood slicking his hair hides spots of gray. He is no longer young.
The 'house' he painstakingly built, brick by brick, tile by tile, all by himself for half his life, is now no more than himself and the stone.
Have I come at a bad time?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I'm afraid I can't offer you hospitality this time around.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: Fate is a cruel, cruel mistress...
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Here for the stone again?
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: At first, yes, but...
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: How do you feel now? Do you believe it to be an ordinary stone?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I believe...
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: It's a totally ordinary stone, and I'm a totally ordinary person. A gust of wind and some drizzle is all it takes to bury us in mud.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I've met many people before, but very few of them were like you, dragging yourself out of abject poverty through sheer stubbornness and grit to achieve greatness.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Even fewer can... No, nearly nobody your age can crawl back up after losing everything in a flash.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Are you saying I should just resign myself to fate and abandon it all? Including the stone?
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: No... I simply wished to ask you one thing: Have you given up?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Of course not!
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I've crawled out of the gutter once, and I can do it again... I must crawl out!
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: The middle-aged man seems to have set his mind on something. He lifts up the box, and speaks to the kindly man solemnly.
You always wanted this stone, didn't you? I'll give it to you, but I'm not abandoning it. I'll come to redeem it one day.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: You mean this is to be collateral?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: That's right. With it, I'll borrow some money from you.
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I'll plant saplings where the trees were burnt down and lay new bricks where the walls collapsed. Right, I might be getting old, but I'm not dead yet!
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: It's just a fresh start. I've done it all once, and I can do it all again...
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: But this time, I'll do it even better!
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: Hmm, I understand. But... Ah, never mind.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Hah, don't tell me you'll only accept it if I give it to you for nothing?
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I wouldn't accept it if you did.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: What I want is a totally ordinary stone. You haven't let go of your obsession with it, so this stone would be too heavy for me.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: The kindly man puts down the basket on his back and sifts through it. From amongst many artifacts of porcelain and jade, he pulls out a fowlbeast crafted of pure gold.
From that golden fowlbeast, he plucks many feathers, also made of purest gold. They are heavy, and clink together with a crisp melody.
Here.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Why are you helping me?
attributes.name: Frustrated Middle-Aged Man

attributes.content: I am accustomed to the road here. On whichever day I feel like seeing if the stone is still there or not, I need only walk two paces to come over.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: But if you were to fall into ruin and wander off with that stone, I would not know where to look for it. And that would be rather troublesome for me, don't you think?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: According to the elderly gentleman yesterday, this should be the area where he found the stone.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The river has become a graveyard... Even the trees that only sprouted after the deluge seem decades old.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: There's someone here...?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Hey, I told you you should've come with us.
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Look at me, I became a mandarin a long time ago. It doesn't matter if it's flowers and trees or vases and pots; in Garden of Grotesqueries, when they see me, they have to bow with respect and call me—
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Wh-What do they call you?
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: 'The Young Minister of Rains!' Heh, I told you, where it rains and where it doesn't rain in Garden of Grotesqueries is all up to me.
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: I simply need to jump in a pond and—Pow! There shall be rain!
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Sounds really impressive.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: What about you? What ministry are you with? What do others call you?
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Nothi... W-Wait! They call me... 'Great Hero'!
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: Great Hero? What ministry is that under? How come I've never heard of you?
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: The Great Hero is... is a big wooden box. When I'm inside it, nobody can open it!
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: Th-That does sound rather impressive... But I'm a Young Minister, so why are you a 'Great' Hero? That's not right! When you come with me to the Garden of Grotesqueries, you shall be called 'Young Hero'!
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Oh, okay...
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: Look at you, did you just wake up? I'll tell you something. Once, we were both in a basket carried on someone's back. The basket's master was carrying us through the river, when you suddenly fell out of the basket—
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Tell me, what were you thinking then?
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: I... I don't really remember.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: Then do you at least remember how you 'woke up'? All of our siblings who were brought back to the Garden of Grotesqueries were awakened by the master. Who knew there'd be someone outside the Garden powerful enough to make even willful stones bow their heads!
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: What do you mean, powerful... He's just a stinky rock! And he's stupid and mean too!
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: On rainy days when you're supposed to rest, he runs out to the fields. When other workers have gone home already, he goes to the river to dig up rocks while his tummy's rumbling.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: He said he wants to build a new house! He's got no money to get people to lay the foundations, so he's doing it himself with a shovel. He can't afford bricks, so he makes his own out of yellow clay.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: It took him seven or eight years to build that home by himself, but he still wasn't done. As soon as it was finished, he was out the door to go do business.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: What an idiot! Stupid rock!
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: He worked so hard on that business to get results, but once he got robbed, he had nothing left!
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: *sigh* So he did all that hard work for nothing?
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: He could have settled down and lived out the rest of his years earning an honest living. But he just wouldn't give up, and put himself through all that torture again—
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: But he's finished now that he's sick... His children are unfilial, and the family and friends who visit just want his stuff.
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: What really makes me mad is that right now, he's still blaming himself for not looking after the box! He still thinks he lost the stone!
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: It sounds like... you're a bit angry at him, but you also pity him a little? If that's the case, why did you run out here instead of staying with him?
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Because I... I don't really know... Why does he never know when to give up?
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: I heard from some friends from outside the Garden of Grotesqueries that things like people fall into holes really easily and are unable to climb out their entire lives.
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: If that's the case, then come with me to the Garden of Grotesqueries. I'll introduce you to my brothers and sisters. They all know loads of stuff, so they can definitely help you figure things out!
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Garden of Grotesqueries... What kind of place is it? Is it fun there...?
attributes.name: Confused Child

attributes.content: Of course it's fun! You definitely won't regret coming with me! I'm telling you, that place has—
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: This is bad, someone's here.
attributes.name: Brash Child

attributes.content: Wait a moment—!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Lin Qingyan reveals herself, stepping towards the big tree. The two young voices cut off abruptly.
There is nothing behind the tree, let alone a child. She looks under the bushes hidden in the tree's shadow, finding a smooth stone lying there, as quiet as a rock.
Transformed by obsession, as if become an object possessed...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: What sort of obsession has ingrained itself into this stone, I wonder...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Have you called us here because you have found the stone, Tianshi?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Indeed.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Not only have I found the stone, but I have also identified the robber who stole into your manse.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Please don't keep us in suspense, then. Tell us what happened, quick.
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: There is no need to rush. I require a cooperative partner in deduction to explain how this stone was lost, and what connection the robber has to it.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: If I may trouble you to stand here.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The man is reluctant, but ultimately acquiesces to Lin's command, standing next to the towering cabinet.
Now, use your key to open this cabinet's lock.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: What sort of trickery are you up to...?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Cooperate with the Tianshi, brother.
attributes.name: Reserved Man

attributes.content: Don't dawdle. Just do as she says so we can all be at ease.
attributes.name: Refined Woman

attributes.content: ...Hmph.
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: In the end, the man continues to follow her command, taking a key from his pocket to unlock the cabinet.
Now what?
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Yes, such an act would not be difficult under normal circumstances...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: But I fear it would be hard to be so nimble in working the lock if it were a dark and stormy night. A few knocks and smacks are to be expected.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Qingyan gives the cabinet a light push. A box atop it falls, smacking the man on the head before it clatters against the floor.
The box smashes open, and from it rolls a sparkling, smooth stone. She steps forward, picking it up before it rolls out of the storeroom.
In truth, the robber was not after the stone, but rather Master Wan's legacy, locked within this cabinet.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: And in knowing where this house's true treasures were stored, they could no longer hide their avarice—
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: You... Get to the point already!
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: (Whispers) I am no mandarin of the Judicial Review Office, young master. I have no intention of getting involved in your family affairs. There is simply one thing I must remind you of.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: (Whispers) Under the Laws of Yan, the crime of theft applies equally between parents and children. Where unauthorized alteration of wills involves assets exceeding statutory thresholds, it shall constitute a felony.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: (Whispers) I ask that you act with dignity.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: You... You...!
attributes.name: Arrogant Man

attributes.content: Young masters... Young mistress...
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Master Wan, he—
attributes.name: Butler

attributes.content: Return this stone to him, posthaste.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: You and I both see true. It really is a good stone. It never abandoned you.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: The old man opens and closes his lips, as if he has much he wishes to say, but ultimately, only a single sentence arises:
It's you.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Yes, it's me again.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: That same smile hangs upon his face, the smile he wore ever since the very first time they met, when the old man was but a little boy.
The young boy plucked the stone from the river, lifting it in front of the passing stranger. As sunlight passed through it, it glowed with a wondrous luster.
So many years... and you've visited quite a few times.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: The last time we met, you could still carry a bench to the courtyard door to enjoy a cup of tea with me.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: You've always wanted that stone. How many times have you come to ask for it, and how many times have I refused you...?
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Why do you keep coming?
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Because I really do adore that stone. It also happens to be just what my garden lacks.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: There was never any special reason for it. The beauty of the finest crafts of heaven and earth has never been rational.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: You are... a very strange man.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Every time I saw you off, I thought, surely he won't show up again, right?
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: But you always returned, whether it took ten days, two weeks, three years, or perhaps five. I never understood how you could be so carefree every time you left empty-handed.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Every time I descend from the mountains, I gain all that I see and hear, all that I think and ponder. Even if I do not have the stone, how could you say I left empty-handed?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: All that you see and hear... So, what have you seen?
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: I have seen cool breezes push cirrus clouds, seen fine blossoms and young chicks, and also...
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I have seen interesting men, and interesting things.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Whenever I walk home, I would think: Will he give me the stone next time?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Haha—*cough* *cough*! Keep dreaming! *cough*! You had to keep running over to my place, all because I never gave it to you.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: You call me a dreamer, but are you not the same?
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: True... We're the same.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Once I die, you won't need to keep coming over here. Pity. When my time comes, this stone will fall into your hands.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Not necessarily.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: A man's strength has its limits. Just like how I carried a basket of stones across a river back then. Despite the care I took, a stone still fell into the river, which was then found by you.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Who would have thought you would never let go of that find for the rest of your life.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Haha, ha—You knew long ago I would never let go! That's right, you knew long ago...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Yes. I knew it from the day you stood in the river.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: So... what did you hope to gain by finding me?
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Most times, I simply wished to see it. I adore it, so naturally I wanted a closer look.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: There are many stones in this land.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: But not many that are interesting. To chance upon it is destiny at work.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Thankfully... there aren't many people on this land as stubborn as you, either.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: When it comes to stubbornness, you are many li ahead of me.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I'm afraid it's this stubbornness of yours that allows even willful stones to bow their heads.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: A man has but... a single life. From that deluge... I found my life again, thanks to the stone... and to you.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: I cannot... give up on this life of mine.
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: That's right. You did it.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Despite falling into deep ravines and striking great boulders, you have never once given up.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: I saw it all.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: This stone... I give to you. I give it to you. You...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Since you have already awakened it, allow it to choose.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: Heheh... You... bothered me my entire life... for this stone... Now... I can finally be free...
attributes.name: Bedridden Old Man

attributes.content: Yes. Put it down. You can put it down now.
attributes.name: Kindly Man

attributes.content: The boy stares blankly at the stone in his palm. As sunlight passes through it, it glows with a wondrous luster. The river gently flows past, wetting his pulled-up pants.
He does not see his friends he always plays by the river with, nor does he see the man who always has a kind smile on his face.
There is no wind, no trees, no sky... He does not sense a single thing other than the river.
Time has stopped at the very moment he dredged up that stone from its waters.
(Melancholy smile)
attributes.name: Cocky Boy

attributes.content: The boy opens his hand, allowing the stone to fall back into the river, not thinking about whether it will sink to the river's bed, or follow its flow to the ocean deep.
He closes his eyes, and listens to the sound of the thunder, the wind, and the deluge's roar.
